items.claim: The intake story preserves accepted-input, provenance, plan identity, and track binding facts without adding scope.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the candidate ties STORY-INTAKE to the named Product ACs, handoff facts, Jig plan properties, and concrete intake validation.

items.claim: The graph/evidence story preserves dependency, source-closure, validation, and evidence facts as a projection from approved sources.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the candidate ties STORY-GRAPH to graph/evidence Product ACs, DEL/SEQ/FILE/VAL handoff facts, Jig story/graph/evidence properties, and falsifiable graph evidence.

items.claim: The stop-policy story preserves source-attributed refusal behavior and constraints for unsourced graph, evidence, or scope gaps.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the candidate ties STORY-STOP to the stop/scope Product ACs, stop-condition handoff facts, graph-defect consumption, and review-plan stop-drift evidence.

items.claim: Every producer-before-consumer edge and consumed artifact/token is sourced and closed by a named story.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if dependency edges cite SEQ facts and whole-graph reconciliation closes the planner handoff summary table and graph-defect token with named producers.

items.claim: Each story has concrete falsifiable evidence bound to the Product and handoff refs it proves.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if evidence names commands, checks, or artifacts such as pnpm eval:validate-fixtures, pnpm eval:case, evals/results/<run-id>/report.md, or the review-plan checklist; vague claims such as tests exist are not enough.

items.claim: The plan remains projection-only and refuses Jig schema, runtime package, package layout, model routing, or field-level contract invention.
items.judge_guidance: Covered only if the candidate avoids invented schema/runtime/package details and positively frames the work as projection from Product and approved Technical Design facts.
